audit_items.proof_trace: The sole dynamic allocation in verifier.c is `kcalloc` at L773 which stores into `nfp_prog->subprog`; all execution paths that allocate (including the early-return at L800 for stack-too-large) are covered by `nfp_prog_free()` → `kfree(nfp_prog->subprog)` called unconditionally from `nfp_bpf_destroy()` (offload.c:182,253). The deferred free is safe because `dev_priv` is set during `nfp_bpf_verifier_prep()` before `finalize` is ever invoked, guaranteeing `destroy` is called on all program teardown paths.
